signals.title: Code of Practice on Marking and Labelling AI-Generated Content
signals.summary: The European Commission released a draft Code of Practice to ensure AI-generated content is marked and labelled, aligning with Article 50 of the AI Act. This aims to enhance transparency and accountability in the use of AI-generated content.
key_provisions: Users must label deepfakes and AI-generated texts on public interest matters.

signals.title: Draft Guidelines on Protection of Minors Online under the Digital Services Act (DSA)
signals.summary: The European Commission initiated a public consultation on guidelines to protect minors online, focusing on age verification and content recommendation systems to shield minors from harmful content.
key_provisions: Improved content recommendation systems for minors.

signals.title: Harmonisation of Transparency Reporting Rules under the DSA
signals.summary: The European Commission adopted an Implementing Regulation to standardize transparency reporting by intermediary service providers under the DSA, ensuring uniformity in reporting practices.
key_provisions: Reports must detail content moderation practices.

signals.title: Formal Proceedings Against X (formerly Twitter) Under the DSA
signals.summary: The European Commission initiated proceedings to assess X's compliance with the DSA, focusing on risk management, content moderation, and transparency.
key_provisions: Assessment of compliance with DSA obligations.
Evaluation of transparency in content moderation.

signals.title: Consultation on Protecting Media Service Providers on Online Platforms
signals.summary: The European Commission sought feedback on guidelines to protect media providers from unwarranted content removal by large platforms, as outlined in the European Media Freedom Act.
key_provisions: Platforms must notify media providers before content removal.
Media providers have 24 hours to respond.

signals.title: Revised Code of Conduct on Countering Illegal Hate Speech Online
signals.summary: The updated Code of Conduct was integrated into the DSA framework to enhance the handling of illegal hate speech by online platforms, improving transparency and accountability.
key_provisions: Strengthened measures for detecting illegal hate speech.
Enhanced transparency and accountability for platforms.
